exemplar.title: How have the power dynamics between Spain and Catalonia changed since the 2017 referendum?

detailedExemplarData.title: How have the power dynamics between Spain and Catalonia changed since the 2017 referendum?

strengths: Cohesive, purposeful structure with a comprehensive table of contents and clear headings
Language is generally precise and academic, guiding the reader effectively
weaknesses: Bibliography formatting is inconsistent; several URLs are broken or misformatted
Minor lapses in citation style and occasional reference errors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Topâband performance in organizational structure and clarity (both band 3) demonstrates clear, coherent communication throughout. Minor formatting issues do not impede understanding, justifying the full 3 marks for this criterion.

strengths: Research question is explicit with clear temporal and spatial boundaries
Key terms (power dynamics, autonomy, independence) are defined or implied precisely
Engagement activities are convincingly linked to analytic factors and contextualised
weaknesses: Justification of project importance could engage broader curricular themes (e.g., EU dimension)
Contextual background lacks economic or supranational perspectives
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments show topâband performance in issue clarity and activity relevance (both 4) and strong, specific justification of importance (3). Overall, these combine to demonstrate clear, precise explanation and fully relevant engagement, meriting the maximum 4 marks.

strengths: A basic research plan outlines sequence of activities and cites appropriate sources
Engagement timing shows some foresight (e.g., interviews scheduled after protests)
Engagement findings occasionally inform the analysis
weaknesses: No discussion of ethical procedures, consent, or dataâcollection instruments
Logistical details (permissions, resource allocation) are missing
Limited evidence that secondary research reshaped engagement strategy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands (planning, engagement logistics, integration) align with midâband performance (band 2). The methodology is adequate but lacks the systematic structure and purposeful feedback loops required for top band, supporting a score of 2 out of 3.

strengths: Accurate, purposeful application of course concepts (Lukes, civilâresistance theory, Weber) tied to evidence
Clear analysis of key factors (protest participation, constitutional constraints) with balanced comparison across time
Partial synthesis of stakeholder perspectives, notably in legitimacy and agendaâsetting discussions
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is somewhat surfaceâlevelâeconomic and EU dimensions are underexplored
Stakeholder perspectives are not fully integrated into a coherent overarching argument
Counterâarguments and alternative viewpoints are limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates mostly accurate concept use (band 4), clear balanced analysis (band 3), and partial synthesis (band 3). These midâtoâupperâband performances across strands justify placing the work in the 5â6 range of the criterion, awarding 6 out of 8.

strengths: Reflective evaluation of surprises and methodological choices (e.g., low turnout)
Awareness of personal bias and its potential influence
Adequate reflection on lessons learned from first-hand engagement
weaknesses: Evaluation of strengths and limitations of sources is not systematic
Impact of identified biases on interpretation is not deeply examined
Future implications and detailed personal learning trajectories are not articulated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand (evaluation of research, bias awareness, reflective insight) sits solidly in the midâband (3). The reflection is adequate but lacks the critical depth and futureâoriented insight required for higher marks, supporting a score of 3 out of 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student explicitly states the political question (âHow have the power dynamics between Spain and Catalonia changed since the 2017 referendum?â) and immediately provides concise historical context (the referendum, Article 155, injuries, the symbolic declaration).  â¢ Key terms such as power dynamics, autonomy and independence are defined or implied with precision, showing a clear grasp of what will be analysed.  â¢ The issueâs scope (temporal frame â post-2017) and spatial frame (SpainâCatalonia) are unambiguous, enabling the reader to understand what evidence will be relevant.  Consequently the strand meets the upper-band descriptors for clarity and contextualisation.

A basic methodology section outlines the sequence of activities and cites a range of secondary sources in the bibliography, indicating that some planning occurred and that sources were broadly appropriate.  â¢ However, there is no explicit research timeline, no discussion of alternative sources, nor evidence of systematic note-taking or data-collection instruments beyond the listed questions.  â¢ This fits the descriptor of a âbasic planâ rather than a well-structured, purposeful plan, justifying a mid-band (2) award.

The student identifies dates (1 Oct protest, 12 Oct national day) and explains why the interviews were scheduled after the protest to avoid bias, showing forward thinking.  â¢ Yet logistics (permissions, recording methods, risk management) are not detailed and there is no explicit resource allocation.  â¢ Planning is therefore present but patchy, aligning with the mid-band description.

Key course conceptsâpower (Lukesâ three dimensions), legitimacy, sovereignty, social movements, civil-resistance theoryâare applied accurately to the CataloniaâSpain context.  â¢ Theories are not merely name-dropped; each is connected to specific empirical evidence (e.g., Weberâs monopoly of violence used to explain Spainâs advantage).  â¢ While not exhaustive enough for the 5-6 band, the application is adequate and mostly accurate across the essay; therefore the top of the 3-4 band is justified.

Engagement findings are occasionally used to steer the analysis (e.g., low protest turnout â argument about waning social-movement power; interview comments â legitimacy discussion).  â¢ Nonetheless, the process is one-directional; there is little evidence that secondary research subsequently refined interview questions or that emerging insights altered the engagement strategy.  â¢ Such intermittent linkage corresponds to âresearch sometimes informs engagementâ, meriting band-score 2.

All three engagement activities (attending an October-1 protest, interviewing independence supporters, attending the Spanish national-day celebration) are clearly described and each is explicitly tied to an analytical factor (social movements, legitimacy, sovereignty) and to a distinct level of analysis.  â¢ The narrative shows how observations from each activity feed the subsequent discussion, demonstrating direct relevance, not merely listing.  â¢ Because the links are convincing and fully explained, the strand merits the top of the 3-4 band.

Language is generally precise and academic; key terms are defined and claims are articulated coherently.  â¢ Occasional minor lapses in grammar or referencing do not impede understanding.  â¢ Overall clarity and flow satisfy the descriptor for the highest communication band.

The student goes beyond description by comparing 2017 and 2024 data, analysing protest participation, government stances and constitutional constraints.  â¢ However, the discussion remains somewhat surface-level; economic or EU dimensions are not probed, and counter-arguments are limited.  â¢ This represents âclear analysis; key factors exploredâ but not the depth or insight needed for 5-6, so a solid 3 is appropriate.

The student comments on trying to âmake unbiased observationsâ and recognises that living in Barcelona may shape interest and expectations, showing awareness of potential personal bias.  â¢ However, the impact of these biases on data interpretation is not probed in depth.  â¢ Identifying but not fully exploring bias sits comfortably in the 3-4 range; a mid-score of 3 is awarded.

The essay follows a logical structure with an advance table of contents, clear section headings and smooth progression from context to methodology, thematic analysis and conclusion.  â¢ Each section fulfills its stated purpose, guiding the reader effectively, which aligns with the top communication band.

The student reflects on surprises (low protest turnout) and acknowledges methodological choices (interviews after observation) indicating some evaluative thinking.  â¢ Nevertheless, strengths and limitations of sources or engagement tools are not systematically unpacked, so the evaluation remains partial.  â¢ This fits the 3-4 band but not its upper reaches; 3 marks are suitable.

The student justifies importance by linking the topic to their personal experience of living in Barcelona and to the wider uncertainty and polarisation surrounding Cataloniaâs future; this is specific rather than generic.  â¢ Suitability is also addressed through a rationale for examining three factors (social movements, legitimacy, sovereignty) which clearly align with power-focused global-politics concepts.  â¢ While the justification is convincing, it is not especially nuanced or multidimensional (e.g. no explicit reference to curricular themes), so a strong but not top-of-band mark (3) is most appropriate.

Multiple stakeholder views (activists, ordinary Catalans, Spanish nationalists) are presented and occasionally contrasted, e.g., differing perceptions of sovereignty.  â¢ Some connections are made (government disengagement weakening social movement legitimacy), but perspectives are not fully woven into an integrated argument.  â¢ This partial synthesis matches the mid-band descriptor, warranting 3 marks.

There is clear though brief reflection on learning: the student notes gaining âfirst-hand opinionsâ and adjusting their perception of social-movement strength.  â¢ Future implications or detailed personal growth analysis are absent, so this is adequate rather than in-depth.  â¢ A band-score of 3 matches âadequate reflection; lessons notedâ.

annotations.comment: The research question is stated explicitly and sets clear temporal and spatial boundaries, providing a solid foundation for analysis.
The comprehensive table of contents guides the reader effectively through the essayâs structure, demonstrating strong organization.
The opening paragraph provides concise historical context, but integrating economic or EU dimensions could enrich the background further.
While the referendum narrative is detailed, consider adding broader political context (e.g., EU reactions or economic impacts) to deepen justification.
The personal rationale anchors the study in lived experience; to elevate importance further, link it explicitly to global-politics curricular themes.
The methodology outlines engagement activities clearly, but lacks discussion of ethical considerations, consent procedures, and data-collection tools.
The protest observation is described vividly, but the analysis would benefit from probing reasons for low turnout to check for sample or timing bias.
Sequencing interviews post-protest shows forethought, yet logistical details (e.g., recording permission, translation) remain unspecified.
The statistic on peaceful protest success is useful, but its reliability should be evaluated and triangulated with additional sources.
Bibliography formatting is inconsistent; adopt a single citation style (e.g., APA) and ensure all entries include access dates.
Several URLs in the bibliography appear broken or truncated; verify hyperlinks to ensure source accessibility.
The ActionAid entry contains encoding errors (math tags visible); correct formatting for professionalism and clarity.
The use of Burgen (2017) effectively demonstrates 2017 protest scales; to deepen analysis, explore government motivations and response beyond pressure.
Linking turnout decline to power loss is logical; consider probing underlying causes (demographics, campaign fatigue) to strengthen causal analysis.
Integration of civil resistance theory is strong; however, the U.S. marriage-equality case differs constitutionallyâselect a more analogous example.
The section on legitimacy is well-structured and applies the concept accurately to interview data.
Citing Blatter (2023) clarifies legitimacy, yet referencing a peer-reviewed journal article could enhance conceptual rigor.
Presenting 60% disapproval data strengthens your argument; consider adding a simple chart to visualize shifts over time.
The list of Lukesâ three dimensions is accurate; the analysis would benefit from explicitly applying all dimensions to Cataloniaâs case.
The discussion of agenda-setting and ideological power synthesizes government and movement perspectives well.
The Scotland case provides useful comparison; expanding on the legal barriers to further referendums would deepen your analogical analysis.
Interview findings on sovereignty are clearly presented; contrasting Catalan and Spanish viewpoints simultaneously could enhance synthesis.
Weberâs monopoly of violence framework is applied excellently, grounding the sovereignty discussion in theory.
The claim on illegitimate violence could be bolstered by an additional case study of a de facto autonomy challenge.
The conclusion effectively recaps key concepts but should acknowledge limitations and suggest avenues for further research.
Reflection on first-hand insight is valuable; articulate how these insights will inform your future global-politics investigations.
Standalone URLs are listed without standardized hyperlink formatting; integrate them into citations for consistency.

rubricData.description: For the new Global Politics syllabus
rubricData.name: Global Politics IA: Engagement Activity

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
1. Issue not identified or irrelevant
2. No meaningful explanation
No discussion of why the project matters
Activities absent or unrelated to issue

1. Issue named but explanation vague / superficial
2. Limited or missing context
Importance / suitability mentioned but weak or generic
Activities listed; relevance implied or weakly linked

1. Issue explicitly stated
2. Clear, precise explanation with relevant context
Importance / suitability clearly justified with specific reasons
Activities fully explained and convincingly linked to the issue
No link between research and engagement
Activities planned but timing / logistics patchy
Research consistently informs engagement; findings feed back into further inquiry
Clear, balanced analysis; key factors explored
Effective synthesis; perspectives woven into coherent argument
Biases explored; impact on work examined
Logical structure; mostly easy to follow
